name: Murdock
notes: - Wears a dog tag, which he keeps it hidden on his person at all costs. Claims it contains his name, Murdock. - A slow grower at creation, currently physically stunted from years of scarcity. Young adult, but their voice would make you think otherwise. - Primarily carnivorous, but capable of digesting other types of food. Will eat wings WITH the bone (her stomach is extra fucking acidic). - Likewise, capable of holding breath up to 15min. - Current height [Mission 1, 2]: 4'3"//1.30m - [union pilots, currently working for them]
history:
Life was sheltered, sure. They were never allowed to leave the labs and meet people they spot through cameras and tech, yeah. They were never allowed to leave the premises, undeniably. But for what it's worth, Murdock remembers their childhood with fondness and comfort - something which is sorely absent in the present day.All it took was a single day for the operatives to break and enter its home, slaughter its father and either ransack or destroy his work and projects, something the hybrid entity took no mind to when it needed to flee to survive. A single day to cast it into the lowest pits and tightest passages, to escape would-be captors and avoid detection.A single day to turn fascination and curiosity to hurt and bitterness.


Perseverance. Adaptation. Spite.Survival was his main objective and desperation his drive, it did not take long for Murdock to turn to petty thievery, vandalism and break-ins of his own to attain what he needs to live another day. Hiding at day and using nights for cover, never seeking help as none were spared for him.With age, experience and stolen manuals, the hybrid became a tinkerer in his spare time, using cobbled together explosives to further terrorize the local districts above, both for ease of access and swift escape. Any discarded appliances becoming either dismantled materials or given a second life for a little longer.


Of course, this wouldn’t exist if they remained hidden. Unbeknownst to him, the local rumors eventually reached the Union, leading some operatives to crack down on their presence. A particularly bad day, and Murdock finds themselves cornered in one of their many passageways, surrounded on all fronts.And yet.A sharp scrap pressed against the throat and threats to claim a life is all it took to be acknowledged as more than a vermin . Or maybe he was just lucky the person whose life he threatened had a soft heart, as his father’s was.


A leap into the unknown a second time.A new turn, a shiny new collar to haphazardly mask her, and promises of a new life, a.. “better” life. Between the choice of death and becoming a pilot, he probably should’ve contemplated more on it. For as much as the prospect of being assigned a mech, a companion of his own, convinced him to join, being suddenly thrust into a compact naval carrier, packed with people (who probably don’t want to acknowledge him as much as he doesn’t want to acknowledge them), it is not something he was keen on. Far from it, actually.…At the very least, she can call Sarchosuchus her own. Wouldn’t be the first time she fixed someone else’s leftovers, after all.

reserves.name: Personal Touch
reserves.label: Resource
reserves.description: It looks like Vonna's taken a shine to you, or at the very least taken a shine to your chassis. Your mech gains a free Custom Paint Job for the duration of the next mission, and you can also spend this reserve to gain +1 Accuracy on all mech skill checks and saves during a combat of your choice.

actions.name: Personal Touch
actions.detail: Spend this reserve during a combat of your choice to gain +1 Accuracy on all mech skill checks and saves for that combat.

MechSystems.name: COMP/CON-Class Assistant Unit
MechSystems.effect:
Your mech has a basic COMP/CON unit, granting it the AI tag. The COMP/CON can speak to you and has a personality, but, unlike an NHP, is not truly capable of independent thought. It is obedient to you alone.
You can give control of your mech to its COMP/CON as a protocol, allowing your mech to act independently on your turn with its own set of actions. Unlike other AIs, a mech controlled by a COMP/CON has no independent initiative and requires direct input. Your mech will follow basic courses of action (defend this area, attack this enemy, protect me, etc.) to the best of its ability, or will act to defend itself if its instructions are complete or it receives no further guidance. You can issue new commands at the start of your turn as long as you are within Range 50 and have the means to communicate with your mech. Comp/con units are not true NHPs and thus cannot enter cascade.

actions.name: Engage COMP/CON
actions.detail: You give control of your mech to its COMP/CON, allowing your mech to act independently on your turn with its own set of actions. Unlike other AIs, a mech controlled by a COMP/CON has no independent initiative and requires direct input. Your mech will follow basic courses of action (defend this area, attack this enemy, protect me, etc.) to the best of its ability, or will act to defend itself if its instructions are complete or it receives no further guidance. You can issue new commands at the start of your turn as long as you are within Range 50 and have the means to communicate with your mech. Comp/con units are not true NHPs and thus cannot enter cascade.

active_effects.name: COMP/CON Online
active_effects.detail: COMP/CON is in control of your mech. You can issue new commands at the start of your turn as long as you are within Range 50 and have the means to communicate with your mech.

MechSystems.description: The GMS Companion/Concierge-Class Assistant Unit conforms to all galaxy-wide standards. These virtual assistants pass even the most rigid Turing-Null assessment criteria and are cleared to operate even in the absence of a pilot.

armor.note: When he REALLY doesn't want to be seen.

weapons.note: A mark of an eye is carved onto its blade.

gear.note: The collar stays *on* when you leave your private quarters, Murdock.
Affixed to his boots at all times. Somehow found a way to attach and hotwire a smaller pair to his gloves for further maneuverability on mechs.

mechs.name: Sarcosuchus
mechs.notes:
-Cobbled and patched up with spare parts and scraps, given its own little flairs as a quiet "shove it" gesture, the chassis has been brought back to functioning and capable order. Junior assisted in installing the voice based notification system.-Personalizations: ???- 1 time use custom paint job system (little girl's drawing of a smiley face // pink)


Destroyed during an attempt to flee the scene, a miscalculated smoke bomb left the mech on its last, desperate strands, exposed.


The wreck recovered, he was given full attention for repairs once he was returned to the mech bay. Brought back to functioning and capable order, with proper acknowledgement and responsibility.

systems.flavorDescription: GMS’s latest intelligent munition system is a self-contained, multi-mode weapon designed to be easily deployable from a variety of chassis. Boasting advanced decision-making capabilities, each drone-tier charge is capable of independent target selection and interception, maintaining regular pilot-in-the-loop protocols to ensure optimal detonation parameters.

Semi-Autonomous Directed Payload Charges – “roller charges” in the colloquial – propel themselves around cover, through corridors, and across uneven terrain, seeking out and detonating near hostile targets. Armory legionnaires have taken to naming individual rollers, but mascot-attachment is unadvisable.

weapon.flavorDescription:
Much like GMS mechs, GMS weapons are reliable galactic standards, made using interchangeable parts and built to withstand almost any conditions. There are three lines currently in production.
The Type-III (T-3) line is made up of heavy weapons, ordnance, and other exotic, specialized, or massive weapons. A broad classification, the T-3 range includes conventional-kinetic anti-materiel rifles, super-rapid cycling pulse rifles, missile racks, cannons, and drone nexuses – “hive”style launchers that serve as miniature factory, hangar, and deployment systems for portable drones. The classification also includes the fearsome “segment knife”, a system that uses flash-printing to produce disposable edged weapons in a vicinity around the user.

Much like GMS mechs, GMS weapons are reliable galactic standards, made using interchangeable parts and built to withstand almost any conditions. There are three lines currently in production.
The Type-II (T-2) line displays GMS’s proprietary “charged” melee weapons and energy weapons.

Much like GMS mechs, GMS weapons are reliable galactic standards, made using interchangeable parts and built to withstand almost any conditions. There are three lines currently in production.
The Type-II (T-2) line displays GMS’s proprietary “charged” melee weapons and energy weapons.

Much like GMS mechs, GMS weapons are reliable galactic standards, made using interchangeable parts and built to withstand almost any conditions. There are three lines currently in production.
The Type-II (T-2) line displays GMS’s proprietary “charged” melee weapons and energy weapons. T-2 charged melee weapons are structurally similar to GMS’s T-1 melee weapons, though built with different materials to tolerate the intense heat generated by their projected plasma sheaths. These sheaths can be toggled on or off, depending on the needs of the pilot. GMS’s T-2 energy weapons, like their T-1 kinetics, are sturdy tools with predictable power scaling, minimal particle scattering, and consistent performance ranges. They feature universal ports allowing them to accept a variety of power sources, from hardline cabling through to “magazine” style power packs

systems.flavorDescription: Autoloader drones are many-limbed machines that assist their team by loading ordnance, maintaining powerline hookups, and cycling magazine-fed weapons, in addition to many other physical tasks.

Semi-Autonomous Directed Payload Charges – “roller charges” in the colloquial – propel themselves around cover, through corridors, and across uneven terrain, seeking out and detonating near hostile targets. Armory legionnaires have taken to naming individual rollers, but mascot-attachment is unadvisable.

GMS’s latest intelligent munition system is a self-contained, multi-mode weapon designed to be easily deployable from a variety of chassis. Boasting advanced decision-making capabilities, each drone-tier charge is capable of independent target selection and interception, maintaining regular pilot-in-the-loop protocols to ensure optimal detonation parameters.
Some weapons require further stabilization for optimal use. With Armory-designed siege stabilizers installed, a mech becomes a stable firing platform for any weapon.

weapon.flavorDescription:
Much like GMS mechs, GMS weapons are reliable galactic standards, made using interchangeable parts and built to withstand almost any conditions. There are three lines currently in production.
The Type-III (T-3) line is made up of heavy weapons, ordnance, and other exotic, specialized, or massive weapons. A broad classification, the T-3 range includes conventional-kinetic anti-materiel rifles, super-rapid cycling pulse rifles, missile racks, cannons, and drone nexuses – “hive”style launchers that serve as miniature factory, hangar, and deployment systems for portable drones. The classification also includes the fearsome “segment knife”, a system that uses flash-printing to produce disposable edged weapons in a vicinity around the user.

The siege cannon is the Armory’s quintessential artillery piece: a howitzer cannon fed by an automated loading system. Typically mounted on long-range mechs deployed in artillery or squad-support roles, siege cannons are capable of both indirect and direct fire as the situation demands with variable ammunition options.
